task: Task 6 — Ground-truth fault injection on live GO2 with HELIX deployed on Jetson

helix_run_window.node_deaths_at_elapsed_681s_explanation: All 3 nodes' per-node logs (hb.log/ad.log/lp.log) end with a clean rclpy.shutdown() trace + harmless 'rcl_shutdown already called on the given context' double-shutdown error and 'Process exited with failure 1'. This is the v3 script's own teardown sequence (pkill -f lib/helix_core/...), not a mid-run failure. The sampler runs a few more iterations after teardown and observes pid=0, which then propagates into the aggregator's 'first_death_elapsed_s' and 'no_oom: false'. Functionally HELIX ran the full 716s window and the positive-control fault was correctly emitted.

success_criteria_corrected.all_three_caveat: values flipped to false in the raw aggregator due to script-teardown artifact described above

robot_pose_during_test: down + damp (body_height ≈ 0.07 m, motors released) — set by Task 5 cleanup

injections.payload: rcl_interfaces/msg/Log{level=40 (ERROR), name='test_inject', msg='Costmap failed to initialize during ground-truth inject'}
injections.expected_fault: LOG_PATTERN (matches log_rules.yaml/nav2_costmap_fail regex 'Costmap.*failed to initialize'), severity 3
injections.notes: Positive control. Confirms log_parser pipeline + faults_echo capture working end-to-end on Jetson.

injections.injection: physical — operator covered LiDAR with hand
injections.expected_failure_mode: HELIX has no LiDAR-rate adapter — anomaly_detector subscribes only to /diagnostics (NOT PUBLISHED by GO2 — see Task 5) and /helix/metrics (no publisher exists). LiDAR rate changes are invisible to current HELIX deployment.
injections.interpretation: Negative result. Concrete hardware evidence of the attachability gap predicted by docs/GO2_ATTACHABILITY_UPDATE.md. Without an adapter publishing PointCloud2 rate to /helix/metrics, LiDAR occlusion cannot be detected.

injections.injection: 10000 std_msgs/String publishes to /test/spam via single rclpy publisher
injections.expected_failure_mode: HELIX subscribes to /diagnostics, /rosout, /helix/heartbeat, /helix/metrics — NOT to /test/spam. Flooding an unrelated topic produces no faults because HELIX is not in the data path.
injections.interpretation: Negative result, but useful: confirms HELIX does NOT false-positive on graph-noise of unrelated topics. To produce a positive flood-detection result, the flood would need to be on a HELIX-subscribed topic that has an established baseline rate (e.g., a /helix/metrics stream from a future adapter).

injections.injection: physical — operator unplugged USB microphone, ~35 s detached, then reconnected
injections.expected_fault: ANOMALY or LOG_PATTERN (depending on whether driver emitted /rosout error)
injections.expected_failure_mode: USB mic is not in the GO2 sensor stack and is not published to any topic HELIX monitors. Even if the host kernel logged a USB disconnect event, that goes to dmesg, not to /rosout. Same attachability gap.
injections.interpretation: Negative result. Documents the second-order attachability gap: even peripheral hardware events do not reach HELIX without an adapter that bridges syslog/dmesg → /rosout.

injections.rationale: GO2 MCU at 192.168.123.161 has no SSH (port 22 connection refused — verified). GO2 internal nodes do not appear in `ros2 node list` from the Jetson (verified with cyclonedds_uri sourced). Without a shell on the MCU and without discoverable ROS 2 nodes, there is no GO2 process to send SIGKILL to. Documenting this finding rather than fabricating a result.

summary.headline_finding: HELIX as currently deployed (no adapters) detects only stimuli that arrive on its 4 subscribed topics. The positive control via /rosout fired correctly with ~1.8 s latency. The 3 physical / network injections (LiDAR occlude, USB cable disconnect, /test/spam flood) produced 0 faults because no adapter bridges those signals to /diagnostics or /helix/metrics. This concretely demonstrates the attachability gap: HELIX needs the adapter layer described in docs/GO2_ATTACHABILITY_UPDATE.md Phase 1 to be useful on the GO2.
summary.claim_supported: HELIX detects log-pattern faults on /rosout end-to-end on Jetson with low latency.
summary.claim_NOT_supported: HELIX detects sensor or hardware anomalies on the GO2 — REQUIRES adapters that do not yet exist.
